summary: Julia Evans announces her article published in Code Words, the Recurse Centre's quarterly publication, about tricking neural networks into misclassifying images. The post is essentially a brief announcement linking to the full article, which includes reproducible code. She also recommends several other Code Words articles on topics like DDoS, Git internals, and floating point equality.
key_points: Evans published an article about adversarial examples in neural networks in the Recurse Centre's Code Words publication
The article includes reproducible code so readers can experiment themselves
She highlights other notable Code Words articles on DDoS, Git internals, and floating point curiosities
money_quotes: It comes with code so that you can reproduce everything in it yourself!
Code Words is overall pretty excellent
key_insight: Adversarial examples in neural networks are an accessible topic that anyone can explore with hands-on code.
